key_points: Bhakti and Sufi movements focused on love, devotion, and personal connection with God
Saints like Mirabai, Kabir, and Tulsidas spread messages of devotion and equality
Sufi saints emphasized inner spirituality, tolerance, and service to humanity
Devotional expression included songs, poetry, hymns, art, and pilgrimages
Movements promoted social harmony and reduced caste-based barriers
examples: Mirabai sang devotional songs to express her love for Krishna
Kabir’s poems taught equality and devotion over rituals
Sufi shrines welcomed people of all religions and communities
Pilgrims visiting Ajmer Sharif or Varanasi temples expressed devotion through rituals and songs
formulas: Bhakti → Devotion through love, poetry, and music
Sufi → Devotion through inner spirituality, tolerance, and service
exam_tips: Remember the key saints and their teachings
Focus on the forms of devotional expression
Understand the social impact of Bhakti and Sufi movements
Be able to give examples of inclusive practices and communal harmony
Learn the difference between Bhakti and Sufi approaches to devotion
